article_meta.title: Four days into war with Iran, at least one of the United States’ Gulf allies is already running low on crucial intercept

reasoning: Multiple failures: uses non-approved bias type labels, includes at least one clear attribution violation by flagging quoted/source language as author bias, several weak/overstated flags (false positives), and misses notable author-prose bias instances. Output also wrapped in markdown fences, violating format.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Flagged Trump's quoted wording ('giving'... etc.) as bias, an attribution violation; other items mix quoted material within flagged text.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Most flagged items are neutral reporting or reasonable context (e.g., citing Democrats’ concerns, identifying Kendall’s role) and not genuine author-level bias.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed clear author-prose elements like 'it's a numbers game,' 'US rivals like China will be watching closely,' and assertive framing such as 'The immediate concern though is... not the US,' which are candidates for Spin/Word Choice or Opinion-as-fact.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: All biasType labels are outside the allowed set (e.g., 'Negative framing', 'Source selection bias', 'Skeptical framing'), so type labeling is incorrect throughout.
